work.name: OhhDenny Services, LLC
work.summary:
We assist small businesses, mostly concentrating on HIPPA, with Cyber Security, Software & Hardware Installation and Updates, and Network Configuration. We also build websites using Content Management Systems like WordPress and technologies such JavaScript, CSS3 & HTML5.

Clients
  • Ray-ville Enterprises, LLC: The IT Guy, March 2025 - Present
    • Acting as an Information Technology and Computing Consultant
    • Performs regular maintenance on WordPress business websites
    • Created One Page Landing site for Ray-ville Enterprise, LLC businesses
    • Created Logos for Real Lead and Home Inspections, Ray-ville Enterprises, Ray-ville Home Improvement
  • Jacob David Properties, LLC: The IT Guy, August 2019 - March 2023
    • Acting as an Information Technology and Computing Consultant
    • Performs regular maintenance on business website
  • Kronick Digital Magazine, LLC: IT Consultant, April 2022 - September 2022
    • Assisting in building NFT assets through the implementation of Smart Contracts
    • Assisting building initial website
  • Caveonix, Inc: Web Developer & Consultant, October 2020 - April 2021
    • Implementing & maintaining APIs with ExpressJS on a project following various Agile methodologies
    • Assisting with technical documentation
  • Ideal Nursing Services, Inc: Computing & IT Consultant, June 2015 - August 2017
    • Performed various hardware task including removing and installing of operating systems, basic cyber security, networking, and troubleshooting of various issues

    • Performed Network Upgrade: For this project, I hired two other professionals to assist with taking Ideal Nursing from a wired network to a wireless network. Measurements of the building were taken to create proper network diagrams for approval by Ideal Nursing. Wires were removed and Wireless Access Points (WAPs) were then installed and configured. All machines were upgraded and/or configured to connect to the WAPs. In addition, a new rack with POE ports was installed in their server room for their server, multi-port hub and router. This created a more efficient working space and alleviated unnecessary clutter.

  • Jacob David Properties, LLC - Selma , TX
  • Diamond Grove Baptist Church, Inc - Franklin, VA
  • O'Berry A. M. E. Zion Church - Franklin, VA

work.name: iHeartMedia, Inc
work.summary:
I participated as a member of iHeartMedia's Music Lab team. I assist in the maintaining and improving of a web application that assist in helping program directors of iHeartRadio stations determine how air time (spins) for songs will be distributed across radio stations. I am working as both a Software Engineer and Data Analytics team liaison on the Music Lab team. Some of my tasks include architecting solutions with NodeJS, automating Amazon Web Services CodeBuilds using CloudWatch events for our MySQL databases, improving technical documentation, and learning both our data analytics and software development stack (ReactJS) for Music Lab.

I conducted a Proof of Concept analysis to present to VP of Data Engineering & Analytics for using an aiWare platform that uses GraphQL. This was an idea to help iHeartMedia automate the analysis of some of its market data like spots and digital impression

Previously apart of the Salesforce team, working with the Campaign Recap application which kept track of iHeartMedia's clients radio ad campaigns. I briefly worked with our Kibana instance in attempt to improve logging. I updated our software architecture. I also performed a technical analysis with Vertione One in using there development platform with GraphiQ that could possible help iHeartMedia automate some of its processes.

work.name: Booz Allen Hamilton, Inc
work.summary:
I have written training documentation for the Air Force serving as an Elastic Stack Subject Matter Expert (SME) creating Elastic Stack training courses. This included modules for:
  • The Overview of Elastic Stack
  • The understanding and use of Elasticsearch
  • The understanding and use of Logstash
  • The understanding and use of Kibana The understanding of Beats
I have created learning documentation for server configuration (Puppet) and YAML

I participated as a member of an Agile Coaching team for the Air Force, practicing Scrum using Sprints. I wrote guidance for development teams such as how-to-guides and best practices for use of Agile methodologies.

I served as a member of the DoD community working as an Application Lead/Senior Developer working with and managing Amazing Web Services (AWS) EC2 instances building a Java application that would create and maintain IT disaster recovery plans.

I lead and participated on projects working with technologies such as PostgreSQL, MongoDB, ExpressDB, AngularJS and NodeJS (MEAN) web development, Java and other Microsoft technologies.

Internally, for Booz Allen Hamilton, I have configured and implemented a prototype website using Drupal with Acquia cloud.

work.name: Sevatec, Inc
work.summary:
I fulfilled the role of Web Developer as a member of the Implementation Team for Content Management System (CMS), redesigning of the US Embassy websites at the IIP bureau of the Department of State. We were part of the UI/UX team with this task. We conducted wireframe testing, user interviews, and analysis on the both tasks.

As a web developer, I was responsible for the development of websites and mobile applications on Amazon Web Services (AWS) via WordPress, assisting with technical documentation and end-user training & development transition plans. I participated in weekly calls with WordPress VIP and Automattic (creators and founders of WordPress) to receive guidance towards implementing possible solutions for the client. I also created and implemented the US Embassy Travel Alert Wordpress plugin which incorporates travel warnings and alerts using a JSON file from travel.state.gov.

work.name: Buchanan & Edwards, Inc
work.summary: I fulfilled the role of Application Lead, SharePoint Administrator & Software Developer on the Application Programming Development team for Department of State's IRM/OPS/SIO/EPI Division. I developed and maintained features for Java and ASP.NET/C# based applications for Web.TATEL, Department of State’s JAVA web-based Human Resource Time & Attendance payroll application, and Post Application Software Suite (PASS)

work.name: Creative Information Technology, Inc
work.summary: I fulfilled the role of Application Lead, SharePoint Administrator & Software Developer on the Application Programming Development team for Department of State's IRM/OPS/SIO/EPI Division. work.name: Intepros Federal Consulting, Inc
work.summary: I was a technical consultant supporting the Joint Chiefs of Staff (JCS), Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO)at the Pentagon. Apart of the applications engineering team under the Systems Requirements and System Integration (SRSI) area for the JCS OCIO. I consulted directly with the client in gathering requirements and participating/leading application demos with assistance of using Gartner references for analysis & research into workflow tools. I wrote documentation for a small data inventory system and implemented an application with another developer using ASP.NET/C#. I was also tasked with SharePoint 2003 administration tasks and requests. I worked with a SharePoint developer to create a weekly status automation tool using MS Access and VB.

work.name: IBM, Inc
work.summary:
Projects
  • Web Developer/Database Analyst at National Defense University (NDU), February 2009 - September 2009

    • I worked out of the Chief Information Office (CIO) with the Department of Defense at the National Defense University (NDU). There, I was a part of the Application team and specifically, the technical laison on Data Enterprise System(DES) team for the North Campus of NDU at Fort McNair. I met with professors and officers of the campus to gather requirements for the DES system. I also acted as HelpDesk support by creating new accounts in DES for all those authorized. I coordinated meetings, presentation and briefings with NDU clients on solutions, progress and future endeavors for DES as well as performed assigned technical tasks including system analysis, programming, product installation, testing and system integration.

  • Java Developer/Web Developer at Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA), August 2007 - January 2009

    • I was a part of the Agile Systems Development (ASD) team on the FEMA RIMS team. We provided a web-based, Recovery Information Management System (RIMS), designed to help FEMA employees and disaster victims seek disaster-related information by increasing data accuracy, expanding data access, and streamlining reporting processes.. Our team implemented solutions in an agile environment using up-to-date technologies in J2EE, including Spring and Hibernate frameworks, JSTL, and Oracle. Some of my other responsibilities included gathering analysis of functional and technical requirements, assisting with IT architect design of applications, technical documentation, understanding relational database (Oracle DB) and schema created for applications, as well as mentoring other developers.

volunteer.summary:
Sharing my career and college experience along with mentoring Computer Science students of underserved communities

- Panelist in a Virtual Panel for "Work From Home Best Practices"
- Volunteered as a resume reviewer for Computer Science students

Our mission is to transform individual lives and whole communities through Hip-Hop. We do this through public art creation, teaching courses in the core elements of Hip-Hop, publishing a global academic journal on Hip-Hop, sending artists abroad and hosting Teach-Ins and retreats to build the capacity of scholars, artists and arts educators and arts managers from throughout the country and around the world.
